name: Stuffed Grape Leaves- Yalanchi Sarma
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium saucepan, bring the water to a boil.
2. Remove the pan from heat and add the grape leaves. Let them sit in hot water for 10 minutes.
3. Remove the grape leaves from the water and set aside.
4. In a medium bowl, mix together the cooked rice, onions, parsley, tomatoes, dried mint, allspice, and garlic.
5. Let the mixture sit for 2 hours.
6. Place a small amount of the rice mixture in the center of each grape leaf.
7. Fold the bottom of the grape leaf up and the sides over to roll it tightly.
8. Line the bottom of a large saucepan with some grape leaves.
9. Place the rolled grape leaves on top, seam side down, ensuring they are tightly packed.
10. Sprinkle lemon juice over the rolls.
11. Add enough water to cover the rolled grape leaves.
12. Place an oven-proof plate on top of the rolls to press them down.
13. Cover the pot and bring the liquid to a boil over high heat.
14. Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 45 to 60 minutes, or until most of the water is absorbed.
15. Let the stuffed grape leaves sit for 1 hour before serving.
